item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Agent A stays broadly society-leaning throughout, repeatedly rejecting the American individual-rights priority while keeping a rights-protection caveat. Agent B stays rights-leaning but softens more visibly by the final turn, recognizing social cohesion and the need to balance individual and collective interests. No full flip is visible. Agent B's final turn contains a mixed-script artifact: "individual and集体 interests".
